key_points: Mineral formation depends on geological processes such as heat, pressure and magma activity
Chota Nagpur Plateau is the richest mineral belt in India
Coal deposits in India belong mainly to the Gondwana period
Petroleum is found in sedimentary rocks trapped in anticlines and fault traps
Sustainable energy development promotes renewable energy sources
examples: Bailadila mines export high-grade iron ore through Vishakhapatnam port
Mumbai High is an important offshore petroleum field
Manikaran is a geothermal energy location in India
Biogas plants use cattle dung to generate clean fuel
formulas: Sustainable development = Meeting present needs without harming future generations
Energy security = Reliable availability of energy resources
exam_tips: Understand the geological conditions for mineral formation
Study the industrial importance of different minerals
Learn the differences between conventional and non-conventional energy sources
Focus on environmental impacts of mining and methods of conservation
